title: Taxonomy
description: Taxonomy documents overview material for Taxonomy in the knowledge domain. It is classified as a concept page, so readers can understand its topic, scope, and place in the wiki taxonomy. The page highlights What Taxonomy Is, Core Structure, What Taxonomy Is Good At, Taxonomy Versus Ontology, and Practical Example, with examples, commands, diagrams, or references kept close to the relevant explanation. Important search terms include classification, hierarchy, ontology, metadata, and knowledge, helping both humans and generated indexes connect this page with adjacent concepts. The route is knowledge / concepts / taxonomy, and the frontmatter keeps the canonical subject, ontology role, and navigation context aligned with repository validation. Use it for quick orientation, implementation reminders, and deeper research.
